title: Management as AI Superpower
executive_summary: As AI agents become capable of completing professional tasks in hours, the scarce skill is no longer technical AI expertise — it is knowing what to ask for and how to evaluate the output
A GDPval study (OpenAI) found GPT-5.2 models tied or beat human experts on 72% of diverse professional tasks, with humans averaging 7 hours per task — making delegation economics transformative
Effective AI delegation follows the same principles as effective human management: clear goals, specific deliverables, success criteria, and structured feedback
Expertise creates compounding advantage — domain experts recognize quality, give better instructions, and catch failures faster, making them disproportionately effective at directing AI agents
The so-called 'soft skills' of management — communication, evaluation, judgment — are now the hard competitive differentiators in an AI-augmented organisation
the_one_insight: What's scarce is knowing what to ask for. The skills that are so often dismissed as 'soft' turned out to be the hard ones.

key_quotes.text: What's scarce is knowing what to ask for.
key_quotes.context: On why management judgment — not technical AI skill — becomes the competitive moat

key_quotes.text: The skills that are so often dismissed as 'soft' turned out to be the hard ones.
key_quotes.context: On the revaluation of communication, delegation, and evaluation capabilities in the agentic era
